a11yRisks: The current changes do not introduce new obvious contrast regressions, but metadata hierarchy still relies heavily on CSS context rather than explicit component variants.
Diff tabs remain keyboard-friendly, though broader panel semantics are still fairly minimal for richer documentation use cases.
issues: The most important specimen surface is now reusable, but the refactor page's lower ledger rhythm still depends on page-local CSS behavior rather than a first-class primitive or configurable variant.

DiffViewer is more inspectable in Storybook, but the code surface family is still somewhat compact for broader docs/chat/editor composition because the component API does not yet expose richer panel-level metadata or empty-state framing.
Storybook coverage improved, but the new reusable surface area is still stronger on the bestiary side than on the refactor/ledger side.
nextEdits: Extract or parameterize the refactor page's lower ledger rhythm into a reusable Seraph summary/ledger variant instead of relying on page-local alignment rules.
Deepen the code/diff surface API slightly so Storybook and future docs/chat use cases can configure richer file metadata and panel framing without wrapping the component externally.
Add corresponding Storybook coverage for the new ledger/code variants so the kit improvement is inspectable outside the page preview.
strengths: The Seraph kit is materially more reusable because the bestiary artifact-first composition is no longer trapped in page-local markup.
Storybook and example-app parity remain intact because the new surface was extracted through the shared runtime path.
Dark-mode and state styling quality were preserved during the extraction instead of regressing into one-off surfaces.
